exercises.title: Seed Expansion Basics
exercises.description: Generate an orbital schedule from a single seed and verify deterministic expansion
steps: Read core/seed_expander.py and identify the ThreeSatelliteExpander class
Create a seed with 15 values (5 per satellite), each in [0.0, 1.0]
Call expand_seed() and examine the output schedule
Change one seed value by 0.01 and observe how the schedule changes
Verify that the same seed always produces the same schedule
exercises.expected_insight: Small seed changes produce measurably different schedules, but the mapping is deterministic

exercises.title: Noise Sensitivity Analysis
exercises.description: Understand how observation noise affects seed recovery
steps: Read core/noise_model.py to understand noise sources
Run simulations/01_basic_orbital_sim.py and note the SNR
Modify noise amplitude in the simulation and observe recovery quality
Plot recovery error vs noise amplitude
exercises.expected_insight: Recovery degrades gracefully with noise; matched filtering provides ~4x SNR improvement

exercises.title: Detection Threshold Mapping
exercises.description: Map the relationship between delta-V impulse magnitude and detection probability
steps: Read core/detection.py and understand the matched filter approach
Use explore_solution_space.py to sweep delta-V from 0.1 to 5.0 mm/s
Record detection probability at each level
Identify the minimum reliable detection threshold
Compare with the theoretical Pd vs delta-V curve
exercises.expected_insight: Detection probability transitions sharply around 0.5 mm/s; below this, stealth is nearly guaranteed

exercises.title: Constellation Geometry Effects
exercises.description: Explore how satellite period ratios affect communication capacity
steps: Read core/seed_expander.py, focusing on the prime harmonic ratios (2:3:5)
Use explore_solution_space.py to try alternative ratios
Measure channel independence for each ratio set
Explain why prime ratios minimize cross-channel interference
exercises.expected_insight: Prime harmonic ratios create maximally independent channels; non-prime ratios introduce correlated errors

exercises.title: End-to-End Protocol Validation
exercises.description: Trace a message through the complete PHYCOM pipeline
steps: Encode a test message into a seed
Expand the seed into orbital maneuvers
Add realistic noise (solar storm conditions)
Detect impulses using matched filtering
Recover the original seed from noisy observations
Compare recovered message with original
Identify the weakest link in the pipeline
exercises.expected_insight: Seed recovery is the bottleneck; codebook-based approaches could dramatically improve accuracy

exercises.title: Cross-Repository Integration
exercises.description: Explore how orbital PHYCOM connects to emergency management and infrastructure resilience
steps: Read fieldlink/nexus_emergency_management.fieldlink.json
Read fieldlink/infrastructure_assistance.fieldlink.json
Identify three scenarios where low-bandwidth orbital comms aid emergency response
Propose an integration point between PHYCOM and infrastructure decay monitoring
Document the data flow between systems
exercises.expected_insight: PHYCOM provides a resilient backup communication layer when conventional infrastructure fails
